chapter_context.summary: The LORD calls the child Samuel in the night; Samuel receives a message of judgment for Eli's house and is established as a prophet in Israel.

outline.text: The Spiritual Decline of Israel; Samuel Rested Near the Ark
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Spiritual Decline of Israel; Samuel Rested Near the Ark (3:1-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

outline.text: The LORD Calls Samuel and Pronounces Judgment on Eli's House
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The LORD Calls Samuel and Pronounces Judgment on Eli's House (3:10-18), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

outline.text: Samuel Established and Recognized as a Prophet of the LORD
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Samuel Established and Recognized as a Prophet of the LORD (3:19-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

key_themes.title: Sovereignty of God
key_themes.description: God raises up the humble (Hannah, Samuel, David) and humbles the proud (Eli's sons, Saul).

key_themes.title: The Heart vs. Outward Appearance
key_themes.description: God looks at the inner spiritual character, not physical stature or social status.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 3, how does this encourage you to trust God when circumstances look bleak?
How does this chapter prompt you to examine the sincerity of your own heart before the LORD?

cultural_historical.title: Transition to Monarchy
cultural_historical.text: Israel's request for a king represents a major cultural shift from a tribal league led by temporary judges to a centralized dynastic monarchy.
